name: Eriador
description: The temperate lands west of the Misty Mountains — the Shire, Bree-land, and the empty country beyond. Maritime weather from the Western Sea: soft, damp, rarely extreme.

spring.label: A fair morning giving way to broken cloud by afternoon.
Bright spells and sudden showers — a changeable, hurrying sky.
A soft persistent rain that soaks through cloaks by nightfall.
Thick morning mist in the hollows, burning off slowly past noon.
Grey overcast, cool, the light flat and shadowless.
A keen westerly wind, clouds racing, trees in full voice.
A late frost before dawn, melting away under a pale sun.

summer.label: Warm and fair, a few high clouds, the air soft.
Hot and hazy, the distances blurred, little wind.
Sudden summer showers between long spells of sun.
Grey and muggy, the threat of rain that never quite comes.
A hot afternoon breaking into thunder, brief and violent.
A cool misty morning, promising heat by midday.
A full day of steady rain — the Shire-folk stay indoors.

autumn.label: Cold mist lying thick in the valleys, the sun a pale disc.
Low grey cloud and a damp chill that gets into the bones.
A steady cold rain, turning the roads to churned mud.
A hard westerly gale driving rain in sheets — grim travelling.
Bright and cold, the last leaves brilliant against a clean sky.
Showers and bright spells, rainbows over the hills.
The first hard frost of the year — puddles crack underfoot at dawn.

winter.label: Raw and cold under a low grey sky, the light draining early.
A cold relentless rain, the kind that finds every gap in a cloak.
Hard frost, still air, every twig furred with rime.
Sleet and wet snow turning to slush on the roads.
A light fall of snow, settling for a day before turning to mud.
Freezing fog — the world shrunk to a bow-shot in every direction.
Clear, still, and bitterly cold — stars like broken glass at night.
A true winter storm — wind and driving snow, travel ill-advised.
